Title: Post-transcriptional regulation of fruit ripening and disease resistance in tomato by the vacuolar protease SlVPE3

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nutritional carotenoid and major pigment of ripe tomato fruit
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Vacuolar processing enzyme (VPE); cysteine protease family
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in fruit than root, stem, and leaf; increases gradually from mature green to red ripe stages
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for RNAi silencing and transient expression constructs
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal tomato fruit ripening; promotes lycopene accumulation and ethylene production; also enhances resistance to Botrytis cinerea
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi silencing in tomato; lycopene measurement; ethylene assay; iTRAQ quantitative proteomics; co-immunoprecipitation-SWATH-MS; yeast two-hybrid; subcellular colocalization; immunoblot cleavage assay; cell-free cleavage assay with VPE inhibitor; ChIP assay
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: KTI4; mitochondrial glycoprotein family protein (Solyc03g079930); wound/stress protein (Solyc03g093360)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening stage; ethephon treatment; Botrytis cinerea inoculation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlVPE3 expression rises during ripening and is induced by ethephon. 2. RNAi repression delays fruit color change, sharply reduces lycopene accumulation, and lowers ethylene production. 3. SlVPE3-silenced fruit are more susceptible to Botrytis cinerea. 4. SlVPE3 directly interacts with and cleaves KTI4 in vacuoles, linking post-transcriptional proteolysis to ripening and disease resistance.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SlVPE3 does not directly bind promoters of ripening genes tested, indicating mainly post-transcriptional regulation rather than transcription factor activity in fruit. Authors propose a positive feedback loop with ethylene.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing causes delayed ripening, low lycopene, low ethylene, and higher disease susceptibility.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to improve ripening quality and postharvest resistance, but manipulation must balance disease resistance with ripening timing and pigment accumulation.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: iTRAQ proteomics; SWATH-MS interactome/proteomics; accession numbers NA

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Kunitz trypsin inhibitor family; serine protease inhibitor
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in fruit; transcript increased in SlVPE3 RNAi fruit relative to wild type
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit; analyzed during pathogen challenge and VIGS
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pTRV2-based VIGS construct for silencing in fruit; CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for transient expression in tobacco
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to resistance of tomato fruit to Botrytis cinerea and functions downstream of SlVPE3
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-immunoprecipitation-SWATH-MS; yeast two-hybrid; subcellular colocalization; immunoblot cleavage assay; transient co-expression with wild-type versus catalytic-mutant SlVPE3; cell-free cleavage assay with VPE inhibitor; VIGS in tomato fruit; pathogen assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Botrytis cinerea infection; VIGS in immature green fruit; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KTI4 is a direct SlVPE3-interacting protein that colocalizes with SlVPE3 in vacuoles. 2. SlVPE3 cleaves KTI4 in a catalytic activity-dependent manner. 3. KTI4 silencing increases tomato fruit susceptibility to Botrytis cinerea. 4. KTI4 does not inhibit SlVPE3 VPE activity.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: KTI4 is a serine protease inhibitor with trypsin inhibitory activity, but it did not alter SlVPE3 cysteine protease activity. VIGS of KTI4 did not affect fruit ripening.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing compromises disease resistance; no detectable ripening phenotype in VIGS assay.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for improving postharvest fungal resistance without major effect on ripening, though downstream protease targets remain unknown.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: iTRAQ proteomics; SWATH-MS interactome/proteomics; accession numbers NA

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nutritional pigment and fruit quality determinant
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ripening-associated regulator active during fruit ripening
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Analyzed in wild type and rin mutant fruit during ripening
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates SlVPE3 expression and thereby contributes to normal ripening-associated lycopene accumulation and disease-resistance regulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis in rin mutant; ChIP-qPCR showing in vivo promoter binding; EMSA showing direct binding to SlVPE3 promoter CArG-box
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates SlVPE3 by direct binding to promoter CArG-box elements
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Post-transcriptional proteolysis affecting fruit ripening and disease resistance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Ripening master regulator that directly activates SlVPE3, a protease required for lycopene accumulation and defense-associated protein processing
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlVPE3 expression is reduced in rin mutant fruit. 2. RIN directly binds two CArG-box-containing regions of the SlVPE3 promoter in vivo and in vitro. 3. RIN is an upstream transcriptional activator linking canonical ripening control to SlVPE3-mediated proteolysis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SlVPE3 was only partially downregulated in rin mutant fruit, suggesting additional transcriptional regulators also contribute.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports integrating ripening TF regulation with protease targets for coordinated improvement of fruit quality and postharvest resistance.
